2026-07-08 20:43:26

Yesterday - Markets continued to trade in a relatively narrow range as investors balanced encouraging economic data with ongoing uncertainty surrounding interest rates and corporate earnings. While the broader trend remains constructive, buying enthusiasm has moderated, resulting in a period of healthy consolidation rather than a sustained breakout. TurboRegime remained in its RiskOn allocation throughout the session.

Today - The current RiskOn trade reached its fifth trading day with only modest movement. The active position is now showing a return of -0.15%, while the maximum drawdown since entering the trade remains just -0.22%. Although the trade has drifted slightly lower over the past several sessions, the decline has been orderly and remains well within the normal range of day-to-day market fluctuations.

TurboRegime remains firmly in RiskOn, active since July 1. Five trading days into the current regime, there have been no technical developments that warrant a change in allocation. The model continues to favor patience over prediction, allowing the market trend to develop while avoiding unnecessary trading. Any future transition—whether to a more aggressive RiskOn++ allocation or back to a defensive RiskOff posture—will occur only after a confirmed end-of-day signal.

Live Performance Since Launch - 
Since live trading began on April 1, 2026, TurboRegime has completed six live trades while navigating defensive, standard, and leveraged market regimes. Over that period, the live portfolio has gained 32.0%, compared with 14.1% for a buy-and-hold investment in SPY over the same period, producing an excess return of 17.9 percentage points.

Perhaps more importantly, those results have been achieved with only six allocation changes in more than three months of live trading. Rather than reacting to every market headline, TurboRegime is designed to make deliberate, high-conviction decisions based on objective end-of-day data. While individual trades may experience short-term gains or losses, the strategy remains focused on compounding returns over complete market cycles rather than attempting to predict every daily market move.
